steps.title: Set up the equivalent ratios
steps.content:
If the ratio 10 to 2 is equivalent to the ratio 40 to 2k, then the two ratios must be equal:

$$\frac{10}{2} = \frac{40}{2k}$$

Simplify the left side:

$$5 = \frac{40}{2k}$$

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by 2k to isolate:

$$5 \cdot 2k = 40$$

$$10k = 40$$

$$k = 4$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
